description: Update scripts/publisher.py to enforce schedule-aware posting. When 'post --account EN' is run without --slot, it should only publish posts whose scheduled_time has passed (comparing against current time in the appropriate timezone — UTC for EN, JST for JP). Posts whose scheduled_time is in the future should be skipped with a log message. The --slot flag should bypass this check (explicit slot = explicit intent). Also add a --force flag to bypass schedule enforcement if needed. Read the current publisher.py first to understand the posting flow.
notes: This fixes the issue where all 4 approved posts were published simultaneously instead of respecting their scheduled times. The posting_schedule times are in the content plan JSON under each post's scheduled_time field (e.g. '13:00 UTC', '17:00 UTC'). Keep the implementation simple — parse the time string, compare to now, skip if future.
